description: DKIM-misalignment spoof shape: From shows example.com but the only passing DKIM signature signs for attacker-controlled evil.test. The SPF smtp.mailfrom still aligns with From (so DMARC can pass on SPF alignment and the envelope-sender rules stay silent), isolating the DKIM-vs-From comparison. dkimDomainMatchesFromDomain is false and a single low-severity dkim.domainMismatch signal is emitted carrying the From domain, all passing DKIM signing domains, and the mismatching subset. Low severity reflects that ESPs and platforms also legitimately sign brand mail under their own domain.

authentication-results: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=evil.test

authenticationResults.raw: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=evil.test

signals.message: DKIM header.d signing domain differs from the From domain.
